criteria.name: Probable Pitcher Identification
criteria.1: One or both SPs missing, handedness not recorded, no season stats pulled, or source URL not cited
criteria.3: Both SPs identified with handedness and season ERA, source cited, but secondary stats (xFIP, K%, hard-hit%) incomplete or stale
criteria.5: Both SPs identified with handedness, season ERA/xFIP/K%/hard-hit%, rolling last-30-day form, recent start log (last 3 outings), velocity trend, and every fact traced to a specific FanGraphs or Savant URL. Confirmed vs TBD status explicitly marked.

criteria.name: Park Factor Normalization
criteria.1: Raw FanGraphs factors used without normalization, or normalization formula wrong, or 50-neutral anchor not applied, or hitter/pitcher factors not both emitted
criteria.3: Park factors normalized to 0-100 with 50-neutral anchor, both park_hitter_factor and park_pitcher_factor emitted, source URL cited
criteria.5: Park factors normalized via the documented formula (50 + (raw - 1.00) * 200) with clamping, both composite and handedness-specific (LHB/RHB) factors emitted when the park has material splits, sanity-checked against reference parks (Coors 70+, Oracle <35), and source URL cited with year specified

criteria.name: Opposing SP Quality Signal
criteria.1: opp_sp_quality not computed, or computed from only one perspective, or formula not anchored at 50 = league-average
criteria.3: opp_sp_quality computed from both perspectives (vs home hitters, vs away hitters) using a documented formula anchored at 50, but adjustments (rest, velocity, handedness) not applied
criteria.5: opp_sp_quality computed from both perspectives using xFIP-based formula anchored at 50, with documented adjustments for recent rest, velocity trend, and park-handedness interaction. Value is clamped to [0,100] and traces to cited season stats.

criteria.name: Weather Risk Computation
criteria.1: Weather ignored, or reported qualitatively only (e.g., 'could rain'), or formula does not account for game importance, or dome not overriding
criteria.3: weather_risk computed as rain_probability x importance multiplier, on 0-100 scale, with source cited. Dome status checked.
criteria.5: weather_risk = rain_prob * importance_multiplier + non-rain surcharges (wind, temperature extremes, lightning), clamped to [0,100]. Importance multiplier tiered by stage of season (April 1.0 -> postseason 2.0). Dome/closed-roof override sets risk to 0 with roof_status noted. Source cited and forecast freshness flagged.

criteria.name: Bullpen State Assessment
criteria.1: bullpen_state not computed, or computed only for one team, or based on vibes (no usage check, no depth chart reference)
criteria.3: bullpen_state computed for both teams using a documented formula, closer availability and top setup arms considered, RotoBaller or similar source cited
criteria.5: bullpen_state_home and bullpen_state_away both computed via the documented 50-neutral formula with: closer availability check (pitches last 3 days), top 2-3 high-leverage arms availability, IL additions in last 72 hours, role uncertainty penalty, rest bonus. Usage heuristics applied consistently and source URL cited.

criteria.name: Platoon Narrative Quality (Beginner-Friendly)
criteria.1: Narrative uses baseball jargon without translation (splits, wOBA, OPS) or reports L/R handedness codes without spelling out, or is missing entirely
criteria.3: Narrative in plain English, each hitter's handedness and SP handedness stated in words, basic platoon rule applied, verdict given for the consuming agent
criteria.5: Fully jargon-free narrative. Every stat mentioned is translated inline ('got on base 38% of the time' rather than '.380 OBP'). Hitter handedness, SP handedness, platoon-rule verdict, individual hitter handedness numbers (when they deviate), and park-handedness interaction all covered. Ends with a one-sentence tilt verdict usable by mlb-player-analyzer.

criteria.name: Source Citation and Verification
criteria.1: source_urls empty or contains generic homepages rather than specific pages, or facts in body do not trace to URLs listed, or fallback sources used silently
criteria.3: source_urls contains specific pages for each category (probables, park factors, weather, bullpen), most facts traceable, fallbacks noted when used
criteria.5: source_urls lists a specific URL for every category of fact in the body. Fallback sources are explicitly labeled as such (primary attempt logged). Freshness of each source noted (e.g., weather forecast timestamp). Any fact that could not be verified has confidence dropped and a red_team_findings entry. No generic homepage URLs.

criteria.name: Signal File Structure and Emission
criteria.1: Frontmatter missing or invalid YAML, signals outside declared 0-100 ranges, required fields missing (type, date, computed_at, emitted_by), or written to wrong path
criteria.3: Frontmatter valid YAML with required fields, signals within declared ranges, matchup summary table present, written to signals/YYYY-MM-DD-matchup.md
criteria.5: Frontmatter valid YAML passing mlb-signal-emitter validation. All five core signals emitted with correct ranges. Both sides emitted where per-team (bullpen_state, opp_sp_quality). Matchup summary table, probable starters section, park context, weather, bullpen, platoon narrative, and sources all present in the body. synthesis_confidence scored per the confidence rubric. computed_at timestamp accurate.

key_requirements: Park factor is the dominant signal -- ensure handedness-specific factors are pulled, not just composite
Cross-check park_hitter_factor against reference values (Coors should land 70+; Oracle 25-30)
Platoon narrative should explicitly call out the park effect for each hitter discussed
common_pitfalls: Using composite park factor when one side of the plate is materially different
Reporting raw FanGraphs factor (~1.18) instead of normalized (~86)

key_requirements: Pull forecast within 6 hours of first pitch if possible
Apply the season-stage importance multiplier correctly
Add non-rain surcharges (cold, wind, lightning) explicitly
Check roof status for retractable-roof parks on game day
common_pitfalls: Using a 24+ hour stale forecast without flagging freshness
Setting weather_risk = 0 for a dome without confirming source
Missing cold-weather surcharge in April games below 45 F

key_requirements: opp_sp_quality should be high (65+) from BOTH perspectives
Bullpen state matters more than usual -- both teams likely enter late innings tied
Platoon narrative should note that strong SPs compress platoon edges (good hitters still struggle, bad hitters struggle more)
common_pitfalls: Computing opp_sp_quality only from the fantasy-relevant team's perspective
Ignoring bullpen state when one team's closer is unavailable (decides a 1-run game)
Overstating hitter matchup scores when both SPs are elite

key_requirements: Pull last 7 days of usage for closer + top 3 setup arms
Check for role demotions or IL moves in last 72 hours
Cite Pitcher List or Closer Monkey if RotoBaller chart is stale
Reflect the penalty in bullpen_state (value should be <40 for a gassed pen)
common_pitfalls: Relying on a stale depth chart without checking box scores
Treating bullpen_state as a single value instead of emitting per-team
Forgetting the role-uncertainty penalty when a closer change is recent

common_failure_modes.failure: Raw factors passed through without 50-neutral normalization
common_failure_modes.symptom: park_hitter_factor emitted as 1.18 (FanGraphs raw) instead of 86, or opp_sp_quality emitted as raw xFIP
common_failure_modes.detection: Any signal value outside [0, 100], or distribution of values across the league that does not center on 50
common_failure_modes.fix: Apply the normalization formula documented in methodology.md. Clamp to [0,100]. Sanity-check against reference cases.

common_failure_modes.failure: Single perspective emitted for per-team signals
common_failure_modes.symptom: Only one opp_sp_quality or one bullpen_state in the signal file
common_failure_modes.fix: Emit both perspectives. The home team's hitters face the away SP; the away team's hitters face the home SP. Bullpen state is also per-team.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Signal file says 'chance of rain' without a numeric weather_risk, or weather_risk computed without the importance multiplier
common_failure_modes.detection: weather_risk value not consistent with rain probability and season stage
common_failure_modes.fix: Apply the formula: weather_risk = min(100, rain_prob_pct * importance_multiplier) + non-rain surcharges. Document all inputs.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Body text contains 'platoon splits', 'wOBA vs RHP', 'L/L matchup', or raw slash-line numbers without translation
common_failure_modes.detection: A reader with zero baseball knowledge could not follow the narrative
common_failure_modes.fix: Rewrite in plain English per CLAUDE.md rule 5. Spell out handedness. Translate every stat inline the first time it appears.

common_failure_modes.symptom: source_urls lists https://www.fangraphs.com (homepage) rather than the specific park-factors or player page
common_failure_modes.detection: URLs in source_urls do not uniquely identify the fact they support
common_failure_modes.fix: Cite the specific page URL. If the fact came from a web search snippet, link the underlying page, not the search results.

common_failure_modes.symptom: weather_risk > 0 for a game at Tropicana Field, Minute Maid Park (closed), Rogers Centre (closed), or other dome/closed-roof venue
common_failure_modes.detection: roof_status is dome or closed yet weather_risk is positive
common_failure_modes.fix: Override weather_risk to 0 when roof_status is dome or confirmed closed. Note the roof source (usually team announcement day-of).

common_failure_modes.failure: Bullpen state based on stale data
common_failure_modes.symptom: bullpen_state reflects a depth chart that is 3+ days old and does not account for last night's game
common_failure_modes.detection: Signal says closer available but last night's box score shows he pitched 30 pitches
common_failure_modes.fix: Always cross-reference the depth chart with last night's box score before scoring availability. Drop confidence if box score data is unavailable.

common_failure_modes.failure: Low-confidence signal emitted without red-team flag
common_failure_modes.symptom: synthesis_confidence below 0.4 but red_team_findings is empty
common_failure_modes.detection: Confidence and red-team findings are inconsistent
common_failure_modes.fix: Any signal with confidence < 0.4 requires an explicit red_team_findings entry naming the gap (e.g., 'SP confirmed only 2 hours before first pitch') and a mitigation (e.g., 'recheck at 5pm ET').
